subject: Accounting
title: WeWork's Fantasy Metrics
description: A quick win: understand WeWork's Fantasy Metrics.

hook.text: WeWork's IPO filing introduced 'community adjusted EBITDA'—and accountants laughed out loud.

buildup.text: Standard metrics showed massive losses. So WeWork invented its own measure that removed bad costs.

discovery.text: The creative metric stripped out marketing, general expenses, and even basic building costs.

twist.text: Investors who stuck to traditional financial statements saw the disaster coming from miles away.

climax.text: The IPO collapsed. The CEO was ousted. Standard accounting won over fantasy metrics.

punchline.text: You can rename a loss. You can't rename reality.

quiz.question: Why did WeWork's IPO fail?
options: Financial statements revealed massive losses despite creative metrics
